methods.description: Process for collecting small fragments from a drapery speleothem for subsequent analysis, noting limitations in accessing primary calcite.

steps.description: Sampling was performed in a drapery named DRA-1.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and locate sampling site (drapery DRA-1).

steps.description: Small fragments of the different textures and minerals recognised were carefully collected.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect small fragments from the sampling site.

steps.description: The primary calcite crystals can be observed only in the internal and upper part of the drapery.This area is difficult to access, and its sampling would involve considerable damage to the speleothem.For this reason, the micro morphology and sizes of the crystals were inferred from the study carried out on the altered part of the drapery.
steps.searchable_summary: Document sampling limitations for primary calcite and rationale for inferring properties from altered parts.
outputs: Inferred properties (morphology, size) of primary calcite

methods.description: Procedure for embedding fragile speleothem samples in resin under vacuum to prepare them for cutting and polishing for petrographic analysis.

steps.description: Due to their fragility, the samples were embedded in a resin containing Epofer EX 401 and Epofer E 432 in a vacuum system before cutting and polishing.
steps.searchable_summary: Embed fragile samples in resin under vacuum.

methods.description: Analysis of thin sections of speleothem samples using conventional optical microscopy to study textures and minerals.

steps.description: Conventional optical petrography studies were performed on thin sections of samples of the drapery showing different textures.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform optical petrography on thin sections.
inputs: Thin sections of speleothem samples (prepared from embedded samples)

methods.method_name: Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM) and EDX Analysis
methods.description: Using SEM to observe the surface morphology of gold-coated samples and EDX to obtain semi-quantitative elemental compositions.

steps.description: Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) observations were performed on gold-coated samples using a JEOL JSM-820 6400 electron microscope working at 20 kV.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform SEM observations on gold-coated samples.

steps.description: An EDX system yielded semi-quantitative compositions.
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain semi-quantitative elemental compositions using EDX.

methods.description: Mineralogical characterization of speleothem samples using X-ray diffraction.

steps.description: Mineralogical characterization was done by X-ray diffraction (XRD) using a Bruker D8 difractometer operating at 40 kV and 30 mA, at 2º/min, with monochromated CuKα radiation.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform X-ray diffraction for mineralogical characterization.

steps.description: XRD spectra were obtained from 2 to 65° 2θ.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect XRD spectra over a specified 2θ range.
